title: User Stories
description: Top-level user-story array-block. A user story is a desideratum in the form 'As a <user_kind>, I want/expect …, so that …' (the statement lives in description; user_kind names the kind of user, free text). Stories function two ways, both via relations.json closure-table edges — NOT as inline arrays on the story: (1) as a beginning point for research, exploration, or gap/issue filing (downstream item -> item_derived_from_item -> story); (2) as mandatory verifiable success criteria for work (task_advances_story / feature_advances_story). A story is met when the task or feature advancing it has met its success criteria; a task or feature can't be complete until every story it advances is met. Cross-altitude relationships (feature→story, story→task, story→story dependency, story→gating-item) also live in relations.json edges.

status.description: Story lifecycle. Intentionally divergent from pi-context://schemas/status; reconciliation deferred to a future status-schema unification.

user_kind.description: The kind of user the story speaks for — free text, no enum (e.g. cli-user, in-pi-llm-user, in-pi-human-user).

oid.description: Content-independent substrate-stable item identity (content-addressed substrate identity, Cycle 3). Minted once at item birth via mintOid(substrate_id); immutable across content versions. Optional in the schema so pre-Cycle-3 items validate; stamped on next write.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 (hex) of the RFC-8785-canonical content projection of this item (metadata fields excluded). Recomputed on every stamping write; moves with content, stable across metadata-only churn.

content_parent.description: content_hash of the immediately-prior version of this item; set on a content-changing update, absent on the first (v1) version. Forms the per-item content version chain.
